findings.detail: Surface term is 'IPv6' in all three languages, but the definition body contains ONLY IPv4 context (32-bit, approximately 4.3 billion addresses). The actual IPv6 explanation (128-bit addressing, vastly larger address space, reason for transition from IPv4) is entirely absent. The definition stops mid-context before introducing any IPv6-specific content. This is a content truncation defect affecting all three language variants equally.
findings.recommended_action: Extend the IPv6 definition in jp/zh/en to include: 128-bit address space, 2^128 total addresses, reason for introduction (IPv4 exhaustion), and coexistence period.

findings.detail: Section title en='Relationship between address and IP Address'. JP source '住所とIPアドレスの関係' uses 住所 (postal/physical address metaphor). EN 'address' is ambiguous — could be misread as a network address reference, creating conceptual confusion for learners. ZH '地址与IP地址的关系' has the same ambiguity in Chinese but is standard usage there.
findings.recommended_action: Consider en='Relationship between Physical Address and IP Address' or 'Postal Address Analogy and IP Address' for clarity.

findings.detail: SMTP figure caption: jp='SMTPの概念図' (brief, acronym only) vs zh='简单邮件传输协议（SMTP）的概念图' and en='Conceptual diagram of SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol)' (both expand the acronym). JP caption does not expand the acronym while ZH/EN do. POP figure caption (p484) has similar asymmetry: zh expands '邮局协议' but jp and en do not.
findings.recommended_action: Either expand JP captions to match ZH/EN depth, or strip expansion from ZH/EN for consistency. Suggest expanding JP: 'SMTP（Simple Mail Transfer Protocol）の概念図'.

findings.detail: Three term concepts each appear on two consecutive pages with different scopes: (1) 通信プロトコル on p482 (full) and p483 (condensed exam-style); (2) IPアドレス on p486 (full with examples) and p487 (condensed); (3) DNS on p489 (brief mechanism framing) and p490 (full exam-style). All pairs have unique IDs. Pattern is consistent with intentional progressive disclosure. Not a defect if by design — confirm with curriculum intent.
findings.recommended_action: Confirm progressive disclosure is intentional. If so, no action required.

summary: Chunk 47 (pp. 482-491, sections 14-03 and 14-04: Communication Protocols and Internet Mechanisms) audited across 32 entities (18 terms, 9 figures, 5 sections). All mechanical checks CLEAN: zero U+FFFD, zero kana-in-zh, zero duplicate IDs, all definitions populated in all 3 languages. One MAJOR defect: IPv6 term on p491 has a truncated definition that describes only IPv4 context (32-bit, 2^32 addresses) with no IPv6-specific content (128-bit, address exhaustion rationale) — content truncation in all three language variants. Two MINOR findings: EN section title ambiguity for '住所とIPアドレスの関係' (address vs Address), and figure caption depth asymmetry (ZH/EN expand acronyms, JP does not, on p483/p484). Three INFO notes on intentional progressive-disclosure duplicate terms (通信プロトコル/IPアドレス/DNS). Overall verdict: PASS with MAJOR caveat on IPv6 definition completeness.
